description: Promotional offer combining multiple menu items or products at a special price, presented as a limited-time or day-specific deal to drive immediate purchase decisions.
structural_recipe: Create a carousel or single post featuring: (1) Visual of bundled items stacked or arranged together, (2) Clear bilingual headline (Arabic + English) with enthusiasm emoji, (3) Explicit product combination (e.g., 'Order 2 breakfast plates + get waffles free'), (4) Time/day availability windows (Friday-Saturday, specific hours), (5) Call-to-action with order link or location tag. Emphasize value perception through 'free item' framing rather than discount percentage.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers respond strongly to time-limited offers that create urgency and value perception. Bundling leverages both FOMO (limited availability windows) and collectivist dining culture (meals meant for sharing/groups). Bilingual presentation respects linguistic diversity and builds broader appeal. The breakfast/brunch timing aligns with weekend social dining patterns in Saudi culture.
transplantation_caveats: Time windows must respect prayer times and local scheduling expectations
Bundle composition should reflect actual Saudi dining preferences and portion sizes
Verify promotional legality with Saudi consumer protection regulations
Weekend-heavy promotions may need adjustment for regional variations in weekly schedules
cultural_constraints: Promotions must comply with halal certification standards if not already established
Time-bound offers should avoid scheduling during prayer times (Salah)
Messaging should emphasize family/group dining benefits
